scope: Seeded-defect corpus for the internal detector-regression gate. Each case pairs an AUDIT.json with the defect deliberately seeded in the audited source, mapped to the A-check that owns it. The gate asks one question: after a catalog change, do the seeded defects still get detected? Cases marked authored are maintainer-built fixtures that detect their own seeds by construction, so they earn regression coverage and nothing else; only a recorded real audit run may contribute to a detection rate. These cases are not a sample of real-world repositories, so nothing here estimates reliability on unseen code and no number derived from them feeds a per-repo score.

cases.name: tenant-predicate-omitted
cases.seeded: A board lookup queries by id with no tenant predicate, so an authenticated user can read another tenant board.

cases.name: caller-supplied-selector-unbound
cases.seeded: A caller-supplied email selects the record without being bound to the authenticated principal.

cases.name: control-flag-never-read
cases.seeded: An exports-disabled flag is stored and read into scope but never consulted on the enforcement path.

cases.name: scheduling-uses-utc
cases.seeded: Availability is computed in UTC rather than the entity timezone, so the day boundary is wrong for other zones.

cases.name: money-flow-not-reconciled
cases.seeded: An invoice is marked paid before the provider confirms the charge status, so a failed charge still settles.
